2605.06639.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
2605.06639.paper_summary: We introduce Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a reinforcement learning approach for training recursive agents: agents that can spawn and delegate sub-tasks to new instantiations of themselves recursively. Recursive agents implement an inference-time scaling algorithm that naturally allows agents to scale to longer contexts and generalize to more difficult problems via divide-and-conquer. RAO provides a method to train models to best take advantage of such recursive inference, teaching agents when and how to delegate and communicate. We find that recursive agents trained in this way enjoy better training efficiency, can scale to tasks that go beyond the model's context window, generalize to tasks much harder than the ones the agent was trained on, and can enjoy reduced wall-clock time compared to single-agent systems.
2605.06639.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了遞歸代理優化（RAO），這是一種用於訓練遞歸代理的強化學習方法：這些代理可以遞歸地生成並委派子任務給新的自身實例。遞歸代理實現了一種推理時的擴展算法，這使得代理能夠自然地擴展到更長的上下文並通過分而治之來泛化到更困難的問題。RAO提供了一種訓練模型的方法，以最佳利用這種遞歸推理，教導代理何時以及如何委派和溝通。我們發現，以這種方式訓練的遞歸代理享有更好的訓練效率，能夠擴展到超出模型上下文窗口的任務，對比代理訓練的任務能夠泛化到更困難的任務，並且與單一代理系統相比，能享受減少的實時計算時間。
2605.06639.author: Apurva Gandhi et.al.
2605.06639.authors: Apurva Gandhi, Satyaki Chakraborty, Xiangjun Wang, Aviral Kumar, Graham Neubig
